originalTitle: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
title: Ethiopia's Big Choice: Abiy Ahmed Wins!
content:
Hello, amazing explorers of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with some exciting updates from a very special country called Ethiopia! It's a land full of history, beautiful places, and super friendly people. Today, we're talking about their big choice!

Meet Ethiopia's Leader!

Do you know how in your school, you sometimes choose a class monitor? Someone who helps everyone and makes sure things go well? Well, countries do something similar! They choose a very important leader. In Ethiopia, the leader is called Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. He is like the captain of a big team, and his job is to help everyone in Ethiopia live happily and safely. He has a special group of helpers called a 'party'. Think of it like a sports team, where everyone works together to win and do good things. His party is called the Prosperity Party.

What is an Election?

A few weeks ago, all the grown-ups in Ethiopia had a very important job. They went to special places to choose who they want to lead their country. This is called an 'election'. An election is when people get to say who they think will do the best job. It's like choosing your favorite flavor of ice cream, but much, much bigger! Everyone gets one say, one 'vote'. When people vote, they are picking a leader and a team of helpers. They hope the chosen leader will make good schools, build safe roads, and help everyone in the country. Did you know that Ethiopia is one of the oldest countries in the world? People have lived there for thousands and thousands of years! It's like a really, really old wise friend.

A Big Win for Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed!

Guess what? The people of Ethiopia have made their choice! They voted, and they chose Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed and his team, the Prosperity Party, to lead them again! His team won a 'landslide victory'! That's a super big word, but it just means they won by a lot, a whole lot! Imagine playing a game and winning with many more points than the other team. That's a landslide! This means that many, many people across Ethiopia believe in Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed and his party. They want them to continue working hard for the country. It's like when everyone in your class agrees on who should be the class monitor – it shows they trust that person to do a great job.

What Does a Leader Do for Their Country?

Being a leader of a country is a very important job! Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed and his team will have many things to do. They will work to build more schools so children can learn and grow. They will help build good hospitals so that if anyone gets sick, they can get better quickly. They also make sure there are enough jobs for grown-ups so families can be happy. Leaders also think about building new roads and making sure everyone has clean water and good food. Their main goal is to make life better for all the children and grown-ups in Ethiopia. They also talk to leaders from other countries, like making friends with neighbours, so everyone can live in peace. Did you know that Ethiopia is famous for coffee? Yes, the tasty drink that grown-ups love! Coffee was first discovered in Ethiopia a long, long time ago. So next time you see coffee, remember Ethiopia!

Working Together for a Happy Ethiopia

Sometimes, even in the best families or countries, people have different ideas. The news talked about 'unrest' in some places. This just means some people were not feeling very happy about things. It's like when you and your friend might disagree about which game to play. But good leaders try to listen to everyone and find ways for everyone to feel heard and happy. The news also mentioned 'tensions with neighbours'. These are other countries that are close to Ethiopia. Just like you want to be friends with your neighbours, countries try to be friends too! Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's big job now is to bring everyone together. He wants all the different people in Ethiopia to work as one big team. And he wants Ethiopia to be good friends with its neighbours too. When everyone works together, amazing things can happen! Ethiopia can become even stronger and more peaceful for all its wonderful people. Did you know that Ethiopia is home to some amazing animals you won't find anywhere else? Like the Gelada baboon with its funny red chest, or the Ethiopian wolf, a very rare and beautiful animal! What an amazing place!

Looking to a Bright Future!

So, with Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed and his team chosen again, the people of Ethiopia are looking forward to a bright future. They hope for more peace, more growth, and more happiness for everyone. It's an exciting time for Ethiopia, a country that has so much to offer the world. From its ancient history to its beautiful landscapes, Ethiopia is truly special. We hope that the leaders will work hard to make sure every child in Ethiopia has a chance to learn, play, and dream big dreams. Just like you! That's all for today from your kid-friendly African news desk! Keep exploring, keep learning, and remember that every country in Africa has a unique and wonderful story to tell. Bye for now!
summary: The people of Ethiopia have chosen Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed and his team, the Prosperity Party, to lead their country again after an election.
whyMatters: This is important because the leader and their team help make big decisions for the whole country, like building schools and keeping people safe, so everyone can live happily and peacefully.

originalTitle: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
title: Friends from Africa and Caribbean Ask for a Big 'Sorry' for a Sad Time!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Hello, my wonderful friends! I am your friendly news reporter for Africa, bringing you important news from our amazing continent. Today, we are going to talk about something very important that happened a very, very long time ago. It is a story about fairness, kindness, and making things right. Sometimes, grown-ups talk about big things, and it can sound a bit tricky. But I will explain it in a super simple way for you!

A Very, Very Long Time Ago

Imagine a time when your great-great-great-grandparents were little kids, or even before that! We are talking about many, many years ago, maybe even 400 years ago. That is a super long time! In that very old time, something very sad happened. It was called transatlantic slavery. Don't worry, we will learn what those big words mean together. **Did you know?** Our world has seven big land parts called continents! Africa is one of the biggest and most beautiful continents.

What Was This Sad Time?

During this sad time, some people were taken away from their homes in Africa. They were forced to leave their families, their friends, and everything they knew. This was very, very unfair. These people were then made to work for other people, for no money at all. They did not get to choose what they wanted to do. They were not free. This is what we call "slavery." It means someone is owned and treated like an object, not a person. It was a time when people were not treated with kindness or respect. Everyone deserves to be free and happy, right?

Across the Big Blue Ocean

These people who were taken from Africa were put on big ships. The ships sailed across a huge ocean called the Atlantic Ocean. It is a very, very big ocean, like many, many swimming pools put together! They travelled from Africa to far-away places. Many went to countries in the Caribbean, which are islands with beautiful beaches, and also to parts of America. This journey was very long and very hard. **Did you know?** The Atlantic Ocean is the second-largest ocean in the world! It separates Africa and Europe from the Americas.

What Our Leaders Are Asking For

Today, very important people from Africa and the Caribbean are talking about this sad time. These important people are called leaders. They are like the head teachers of their countries. Our leaders are saying, "It's time for some countries to say 'I am sorry'." They want countries that got rich because of this sad time to formally apologize. An apology is when you say you are sorry for something you did wrong. It helps to heal hurt feelings and show that you understand the pain someone went through.

Why Saying "Sorry" Matters

Why is saying sorry so important, even after such a long time? Because even though it happened many years ago, the sad things from that time still affect people today. Imagine if someone broke your favourite toy. Even if they say sorry much later, it still helps. It shows they know what they did was not kind. It makes a difference. Saying "sorry" helps everyone learn from history. It helps us remember to always treat each other with love, respect, and fairness, no matter where we come from. **Did you know?** There are more than 50 countries in Africa! Each one is special and full of wonderful people and cultures.

Help with Money

Our leaders are also asking for some help with money. This is a bit like when you accidentally break something, and you help your friend buy a new one. It's about helping to make things right. One part of this is called "debt relief." Sometimes, countries borrow money. "Debt relief" means that maybe they don't have to pay back all of that money, or they get help to pay it back. It's like if you owe your friend five marbles, and they say, "You only have to give me three back." The other part is "financial compensation." This means giving some money to help countries that were hurt by the sad time of slavery. This money can be used to build schools, hospitals, and good roads. It helps make life better for everyone now. It helps to fix things that were broken by the unfairness of the past.

Making Things Fair for Everyone

All these requests are about making things fair. It's about acknowledging the past and working towards a brighter future where everyone has a good chance to succeed. When people were taken away and made to work for free, their home countries in Africa missed out on having those people help build and grow their own communities. So, helping with money now is a way to try and balance things. It helps countries catch up and grow strong. It helps everyone stand tall and proud. **Did you know?** The Caribbean is a group of many beautiful islands, each with its own unique history and culture! Many people there are descendants of those who were taken from Africa.

What Happens Next?

Now that our leaders have asked for these important things, different countries will talk about it. It might take some time, like when grown-ups plan a big trip. But the important thing is that they are talking. They are trying to find the best way to make things fair and right for everyone. It shows that our leaders care deeply about our history and our future. They want a future where everyone feels respected and valued.

Working Together for a Better Future

This is a big step towards healing and understanding. It teaches us all about being kind and fair. Remember, even though the past had sad parts, we can always choose to make our future bright and full of hope. We can learn from history to build a world where everyone is free, happy, and treated with love. Let's all hope for a future where everyone says "sorry" when they need to, and everyone works together to build a strong and happy world! That's all for today, my little stars! Stay kind!
summary: Leaders from African and Caribbean countries are asking for an apology and help to fix things that happened during a very sad time called transatlantic slavery.
whyMatters: It's important because saying sorry helps everyone heal, learn from the past, and work together to make sure everyone is treated fairly and kindly today and always.

originalTitle: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
content: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

content: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

title: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
content: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

title: Saka trains with England squad before Ghana match
